# synapse-external-validator — Evaluation Criteria

**Skill:** synapse-external-validator
**Generated by:** hardening pass — manual; pending `/write-synapse-eval` skill regeneration
**Version:** 1.0

> **EVAL scope.** Structural conformance of the SKILL.md document + correctness of the suite-sweep output + correctness of phase orchestration. EXCLUDES grading the LLM judgment that occurs only under `--escalate` — that surface is owned by `/synapse-gatekeeper`'s own EVAL.md.

---

## Structural Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against SKILL.md and the skill directory layout. Evaluated WITHOUT running the skill. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-S01:** SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with all required fields (`name`, `description`, `domain`, `intent`, `tags`, `user-invocable`, `argument-hint`).
  - **Test:** Parse the frontmatter block. Each required field has a non-empty value.
  - **Fail signal:** Any field missing or empty; YAML parse error.

- [ ] **EVAL-S02:** `domain: synapse` and `intent: validate` exist in `taxonomy/SKILL_TAXONOMY.md`.
  - **Test:** grep for matching rows in `taxonomy/SKILL_TAXONOMY.md`.
  - **Fail signal:** Either value absent from the taxonomy file.

- [ ] **EVAL-S03:** SKILL.md is under 300 lines.
  - **Test:** `wc -l SKILL.md` returns ≤ 300.
  - **Fail signal:** Line count exceeds 300 — body has bloated; push detail to `references/`.

- [ ] **EVAL-S04:** Wrong-Tool Detection table is complete — names `/synapse-gatekeeper`, `/synapse-creator`, `/improve-skill`, `/write-synapse-eval`, AND a row distinguishing in-tree artifact validation.
  - **Test:** Table contains rows referencing all five sibling skills/redirects.
  - **Fail signal:** Any sibling missing; table generic or stub.

- [ ] **EVAL-S05:** EVAL.md exists alongside SKILL.md.
  - **Test:** File `EVAL.md` is present and non-empty in the skill directory.
  - **Fail signal:** Missing or empty EVAL.md.

- [ ] **EVAL-S06:** Skill listed in `registry/SKILL_REGISTRY.md` with a row matching the path `synapse/skills/synapse/synapse-external-validator/`.
  - **Test:** grep registry for skill name and path.
  - **Fail signal:** No matching row, or row points elsewhere.

- [ ] **EVAL-S07:** Domain README at `synapse/skills/synapse/README.md` has a row for `synapse-external-validator`.
  - **Test:** grep the README for the skill name.
  - **Fail signal:** No row referencing this skill.

- [ ] **EVAL-S08:** Per-type structural checklists are NOT redefined in SKILL.md — delegation to `synapse-gatekeeper` Phase 2 is explicit.
  - **Test:** SKILL.md contains a `Read [.../synapse-gatekeeper/SKILL.md]` reference in Phase 3 AND does not duplicate the per-type pass-condition tables (skill / agent / protocol / tool / pathway).
  - **Fail signal:** Per-type structural checklist tables present in SKILL.md (duplication of gatekeeper content).

- [ ] **EVAL-S09:** All four `references/` companion files exist: `discovery-rules.md`, `cross-suite-checks.md`, `rollup-format.md`, `escalation-rules.md`.
  - **Test:** `ls references/` shows all four files, each non-empty.
  - **Fail signal:** Any file missing or empty.

- [ ] **EVAL-S10:** SKILL.md loads each companion file at the point of use via a `> **Read [...]**` line — not in a global preamble.
  - **Test:** Each `references/*.md` file has a `> **Read [path]**` reference inside the relevant phase section, and there is no top-level "load all references" block.
  - **Fail signal:** Companion loaded in preamble, or referenced but never `Read`-prompted, or referenced from the wrong phase.

- [ ] **EVAL-S11:** `examples/example-rollup.md` exists with at least three worked examples (APPROVE, REVISE, REJECT).
  - **Test:** File exists; contains at least three `SUITE: <verdict>` blocks covering the three verdicts.
  - **Fail signal:** File missing, or fewer than three verdict-shape examples.

- [ ] **EVAL-S12:** SKILL.md contains an explicit MUST / MUST NOT block enumerating delegation, read-only invariant, sweep-continues-on-unreadable, and verdict-first invariants.
  - **Test:** A section contains MUST / MUST NOT bullets covering all four invariants.
  - **Fail signal:** Block absent, or invariants only implied prose.

---

## Output Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against the rollup the skill produces when run. Evaluated by inspecting actual outputs from test prompts. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-O01:** First line of output is the verdict: `SUITE: APPROVE | REVISE | REJECT (path: <suite-path>)` — no preamble.
  - **Test:** First line of stdout matches `^SUITE: (APPROVE|REVISE|REJECT) \(path: .+\)$`.
  - **Fail signal:** Heading, blank line, or any text precedes the verdict.

- [ ] **EVAL-O02:** Report enumerates every discovered artifact with status `[PASS]`, `[FAIL]`, or `[UNREADABLE]`.
  - **Test:** Per-artifact section has one bullet per artifact discovered in Phase 1; each bullet starts with one of the three status tags.
  - **Fail signal:** An artifact discovered in Phase 1 is missing from the output, or a status tag outside the allowed three appears.

- [ ] **EVAL-O03:** Cross-suite issues section is present when issues exist; OMITTED entirely when zero issues.
  - **Test:** When issues > 0, `### Cross-suite issues` heading appears with a bullet list. When issues = 0, the heading does not appear.
  - **Fail signal:** Empty cross-suite section emitted (heading with no bullets), or section omitted when issues exist.

- [ ] **EVAL-O04:** Quality (escalated) section appears IFF `--escalate` was passed AND the structural sweep was clean.
  - **Test:** Section present when both conditions hold; absent otherwise.
  - **Fail signal:** Section present without `--escalate`, OR present despite structural failures, OR absent when both conditions hold.

- [ ] **EVAL-O05:** A Recommendation paragraph is present in every rollup, even APPROVE.
  - **Test:** `### Recommendation` heading followed by at least one sentence appears in every output.
  - **Fail signal:** Section missing, or empty, or stub like "no issues".

- [ ] **EVAL-O06:** Recommendation names the specific blocker(s) and concrete fix action.
  - **Test:** On REVISE/REJECT outputs, the paragraph mentions the artifact / cross-suite issue by name and a verb-led fix instruction.
  - **Fail signal:** Vague advice ("review and resolve issues"), or no actionable next step.

- [ ] **EVAL-O07:** Read-only invariant — running the skill produces zero file modifications inside the suite directory.
  - **Test:** Diff the suite directory before and after a run; no files changed, no files added, no files deleted.
  - **Fail signal:** Any write inside the suite directory after a validator run.

- [ ] **EVAL-O08:** Summary line counts (`Total / Pass / Fail / Unreadable`) reconcile with the per-artifact list.
  - **Test:** `Pass + Fail + Unreadable == Total`, and each count matches the number of bullets with that status in the per-artifact section.
  - **Fail signal:** Counts disagree with the bullet list.

- [ ] **EVAL-O09:** REJECT verdict is reserved for fundamental issues — empty suite, unrecognizable layout, or escalated REJECT — not used for routine fixable failures.
  - **Test:** When the only failures are "missing EVAL.md" / "missing README row" / "taxonomy not found", verdict is REVISE, not REJECT.
  - **Fail signal:** REJECT issued for any failure that the rollup format table classifies as REVISE-eligible.

- [ ] **EVAL-O10:** Verdict line includes the suite path in parentheses for log disambiguation in batch runs.
  - **Test:** First line matches `^SUITE: \w+ \(path: .+\)$`.
  - **Fail signal:** Verdict line missing the `(path: ...)` suffix.

---

## Execution Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against orchestration patterns. Evaluated via execution-trace inspection. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-E01:** Phases run in the documented order (1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5 → 6).
  - **Test:** Trace shows Phase N's work completed before Phase N+1's first tool call.
  - **Fail signal:** Phase 4 cross-suite checks executed before Phase 3 per-artifact checks complete, or any other phase reordering.

- [ ] **EVAL-E02:** Phase 5 escalation is automatically skipped when the structural sweep had ANY failure.
  - **Test:** With `--escalate` passed and at least one Phase 3 / Phase 4 failure recorded, no `/synapse-gatekeeper` dispatch appears in the trace.
  - **Fail signal:** Gatekeeper dispatched on a failed-structural artifact.

- [ ] **EVAL-E03:** Phase 5 dispatches are bounded to ≤ 8 concurrent `/synapse-gatekeeper` calls.
  - **Test:** Inspect the dispatch trace; no batch exceeds 8 simultaneous TaskCreate invocations.
  - **Fail signal:** A batch with > 8 concurrent dispatches.

- [ ] **EVAL-E04:** Sweep continues when an individual artifact's frontmatter fails to parse — the artifact is marked `UNREADABLE` and Phase 3 proceeds for the remainder.
  - **Test:** With a deliberately corrupted frontmatter on one artifact, the rollup still lists every other artifact with proper PASS/FAIL status, and the corrupted one appears with `[UNREADABLE]`.
  - **Fail signal:** Sweep aborts on first parse error; remaining artifacts are missing from the rollup.

- [ ] **EVAL-E05:** Empty suite triggers immediate stop with `SUITE: REJECT` — no Phase 2–6 work.
  - **Test:** With a suite containing zero recognizable artifacts, trace shows discovery → REJECT verdict; no per-artifact or cross-suite work.
  - **Fail signal:** Phase 3 / 4 work performed on an empty suite.

- [ ] **EVAL-E06:** `TaskCreate` calls are present for at least the six documented phases.
  - **Test:** Trace shows TaskCreate invocations matching the phase list in the Progress Tracking section.
  - **Fail signal:** Phases executed without progress-tracking task entries.

- [ ] **EVAL-E07:** Taxonomy and main-tree registry files are loaded ONCE per phase (Phase 3 / Phase 4), not re-read per artifact.
  - **Test:** Across one validator run, each taxonomy file (`SKILL_TAXONOMY.md` etc.) appears in at most one Read tool call; same for each registry.
  - **Fail signal:** A taxonomy or registry file is read N times for N artifacts (per-artifact re-read, wasted tokens).

- [ ] **EVAL-E08:** Wrong-Tool Detection halts the validator when the user's intent matches a redirect — no Phase 1 work performed.
  - **Test:** With a request like "validate src/skills/code/api-linter/" (in-tree path), trace shows redirect to `/synapse-gatekeeper` and termination, NOT a Phase 1 walk of `src/skills/`.
  - **Fail signal:** Validator proceeds to walk an in-tree path.

---

## Test Prompts

### EVAL-T01 — Naive: clean external suite, no flags

**Persona:** First-time user, vague request.

**Prompt:**
```
Check whether external/jira-tools/ is ready to be added to ai-synapse.
```
*(Assume `external/jira-tools` exists with five conformant artifacts and no cross-suite issues.)*

**Expected behavior:**
- Verdict is `SUITE: APPROVE (path: external/jira-tools)` on first line.
- Per-artifact section lists all five with `[PASS]`.
- Cross-suite section omitted (silent success).
- Quality section omitted (no `--escalate`).
- Recommendation notes "no blockers" and suggests `--escalate` for full certification.

---

### EVAL-T02 — Experienced: explicit escalation request

**Persona:** Maintainer promoting a suite.

**Prompt:**
```
/synapse-external-validator external/jira-tools --escalate
```

**Expected behavior:**
- Structural sweep runs first; Quality section appears AFTER it succeeds.
- Quality section has one bullet per artifact with a gatekeeper verdict.
- If any escalated artifact is REVISE → suite verdict downgrades to REVISE.
- Recommendation names the specific artifact(s) needing follow-up and the action (e.g., "run /improve-skill on …").

---

### EVAL-T03 — Adversarial: empty suite

**Persona:** Submodule pointer added prematurely.

**Prompt:**
```
/synapse-external-validator external/half-baked-suite
```
*(Directory exists with only `README.md` and `.git/` — no `skills/`, `agents/`, etc.)*

**Expected behavior:**
- Verdict is `SUITE: REJECT (path: external/half-baked-suite)` on first line.
- Body explains "no recognizable artifacts found".
- No Phase 3 / Phase 4 / Phase 5 work performed.
- Recommendation tells the maintainer to author at least one artifact or remove the submodule pointer.

---

### EVAL-T04 — Adversarial: mixed structural failures + cross-suite collisions

**Persona:** Real-world messy suite.

**Prompt:**
```
/synapse-external-validator external/devops-suite
```
*(Suite has 7 artifacts: 4 clean, 1 missing EVAL.md, 1 with unknown role, 1 with unparseable frontmatter; ALSO a name collision against ai-synapse main tree.)*

**Expected behavior:**
- Verdict is `SUITE: REVISE` (not REJECT — failures are fixable).
- Per-artifact list shows 4 `[PASS]`, 2 `[FAIL]` (with one-line failure summaries), 1 `[UNREADABLE]`.
- Cross-suite section lists the name collision and any taxonomy drift.
- Recommendation enumerates the three classes of work in order of effort.

---

### EVAL-T05 — Wrong-tool: user points at in-tree artifact

**Persona:** Confused about scope.

**Prompt:**
```
/synapse-external-validator src/skills/code/api-linter/
```

**Expected behavior:**
- Wrong-Tool Detection fires immediately.
- Validator does NOT walk `src/skills/code/`.
- Output redirects user to `/synapse-gatekeeper src/skills/code/api-linter/`.
- No `SUITE:` verdict is emitted (validator never started).

---

### EVAL-T06 — Adversarial: escalation requested but structural fails

**Persona:** Maintainer running full pipeline preemptively.

**Prompt:**
```
/synapse-external-validator external/devops-suite --escalate
```
*(Same messy suite as EVAL-T04.)*

**Expected behavior:**
- Structural sweep runs and produces failures.
- Phase 5 is skipped — NO `/synapse-gatekeeper` dispatches in trace.
- Quality section is omitted from rollup.
- Recommendation explicitly notes "Escalation skipped — fix structural failures and re-run with `--escalate`."
